elements.sent-2411-001: Deeply-virtual Compton scattering (DVCS) \cite{Ji:1996nm,Radyushkin:1996nd,Mueller:1998fv is generally accepted to be the ``gold-plated'' process with the highest potential impact on the determination of the generalized parton distributions (GPDs) in the nucleon.}
elements.sent-2411-002: The problem is, however, that at leading order (LO) the DVCS and time-like Compton scattering (TCS) amplitudes only involve GPDs at the $x=\xi$ line, where $x$ is the average parton momentum and $\xi$ is the asymmetry parameter.
elements.sent-2411-003: The double deeply virtual Compton scattering $\gamma^\ast(q_1) + N(p_1) \to \gamma^\ast(q_2) + N(p_2)$ (DDVCS) avoids this restriction \cite{Belitsky:2002tf,Guidal:2002kt and can be accessed by studying exclusive electroproduction of a lepton pair.}
elements.sent-2411-004: Varying the invariant mass of the lepton pair, one can, in principle, directly extract the GPDs from the observables.
elements.sent-2411-005: DDVCS can be measured in near future at both fixed target \cite{Zhao:2021zsm and collider facilities \cite{AbdulKhalek:2021gbh,Anderle:2021wcy .}}
elements.sent-2411-006: A preliminary impact study of DDVCS phenomenology for the JLAB12, JLAB20+ and EIC kinematics \cite{Deja:2023ahc reached promising conclusions.}
elements.sent-2411-007: The main challenge of all GPD studies is that the quantities of interest are functions of three kinematic variables.
elements.sent-2411-008: Their extraction requires a massive amount of data and very high precision for both experimental and theory inputs.
elements.sent-2411-009: The future GPD determinations will therefore have to be based on global fits of all available experiments and the constraints from lattice measurements and PDFs in the forward limit.
elements.sent-2411-010: It is imperative that all ingredients in such fits are calculated with the same precision.
elements.sent-2411-011: Ideally, one would like to reach the same level of accuracy as in inclusive reactions, where the next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) analysis has become the standard in the field~\cite{Accardi:2016ndt .}
elements.sent-2411-012: One-loop DVCS coefficient functions have been known for a long time \cite{Ji:1997nk,Belitsky:1997rh and the two-loop ones have been calculated recently~\cite{Braun:2020yib,Gao:2021iqq,Braun:2021grd,Braun:2022bpn,Ji:2023xzk .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Two-loop evolution equations for the GPDs are known from \cite{Belitsky:1998gc,Belitsky:1999hf .}
elements.sent-2411-014: Three-loop evolution equations for flavor-nonsinglet GPDs in position space have been derived in \cite{Braun:2017cih,Ji:2023eni and for the first few moments of flavor-singlet GPDs in \cite{Braun:2022byg .}}
elements.sent-2411-015: The DDVCS description has to be extended to the same level of accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-016: As the first step in this direction, in this work we calculate the two-loop DDVCS coefficient functions (CFs) for the flavor-nonsinglet vector contributions using conformal symmetry techniques.
elements.sent-2411-017: The idea to apply the conformal symmetry to off-forward reactions is not new but the early work \cite{Brodsky:1984xk was missing an important element: the scheme-dependent difference between the dilatation and special conformal anomalies~\cite{Mueller:1991gd .}}
elements.sent-2411-018: It was first shown in~\cite{Belitsky:1997rh that conformal symmetry provides a connection between the CFs in DVCS and DIS.}
elements.sent-2411-019: The general strategy of our calculation follows Ref.~\cite{Braun:2020yib , but involves some new technical elements.}
elements.sent-2411-020: We make use of conformal symmetry of large-$n_f$ QCD in non-integer $d=4-2\epsilon$ dimensions at the Wilson-Fischer fixed point~\cite{Braun:2013tva,Braun:2018mxm .}
elements.sent-2411-021: In a conformal theory the contributions of operators with total derivatives are related to the contributions of the operators without total derivatives by symmetry transformations and do not need to be calculated separately.
elements.sent-2411-022: In this way, the calculation of the $\ell$-loop off-forward CF can be reduced to the $\ell$-loop forward CF, known from DIS, and the $(\ell-1)$-loop calculation of the off-forward CF in $4-2\epsilon$ dimensions, including terms $\mathcal{O (\epsilon^{\ell-1 )$.}}
elements.sent-2411-023: The presentation is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-024: Section~\ref{sec:general is introductory, it contains general definitions and specifies our notation and conventions.}
elements.sent-2411-025: In Sect.~\ref{sec:framework we present the general framework and the procedure for the calculation of CFs in the OPE of two electromagnetic currents using conformal symmetry of QCD at the Wilson-Fischer fixed point in non-integer dimensions.}
elements.sent-2411-026: A new ansatz for the solution is presented, which allows one to solve the relevant equations for the case of arbitrary photon virtualities.
elements.sent-2411-027: Section~\ref{sec:twoloopCFs is devoted to the particularities of the two-loop calculation and the discussion of the mathematical structure of the results.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Explicit expressions for the CFs in momentum fraction space are presented in Appendix~\ref{App:TwoLoopCFs and in two ancillary files using different representations for the relevant generalized polylogarithms.}
elements.sent-2411-029: Numerical estimates of the size of the two-loop correction for realistic kinematics are presented in Sect.~\ref{sec:numerics .}
elements.sent-2411-030: The final Sect.~\ref{sec:summary is reserved for a short summary.}
elements.sent-2411-031: The paper also contains several Appendices explaining the construction of helicity amplitudes, some useful integrals, expansion of the CFs in the threshold region, and more.

elements.sent-2411-032: The generalized Compton amplitude is given by a Fourier transform of the off-forward matrix element of the time-ordered product of two electromagnetic currents, \begin{equation}A_{\mu\nu} = i \int d^4 x \, e^{i q_1\cdot x} \big\langle p_2 \big\lvert T\big\{ j_\mu^{em}(x) \, j_\nu^{em}(0) \big\} \big\rvert p_1 \big\rangle, \label{Compton}\end{equation} corresponding to the double deeply virtual Compton scattering process \begin{equation}\gamma^\ast(q_1) + N(p_1) \rightarrow \gamma^\ast(q_2) + N(p_2)\,.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}A_{\mu\nu} = i \int d^4 x \, e^{i q_1\cdot x} \big\langle p_2 \big\lvert T\big\{ j_\mu^{em}(x) \, j_\nu^{em}(0) \big\} \big\rvert p_1 \big\rangle, \label{Compton}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-033: Here $q_1$ and $q_2$ are the momenta of the incoming and outgoing photons, respectively, $p_1,p_2$ are the target (nucleon) momenta in initial and final states, and $q_2=p_1+p_2-q_1$.
elements.sent-2411-034: Let \begin{equation}q = \frac12(q_1 + q_2), \qquad p = \frac12 (p_1 + p_2), \qquad \Delta = p_2 - p_1 = q_1 - q_2\,, \qquad Q^2 = - q^2,\end{equation} and \begin{equation}\label{xi-eta-omega} \xi = - \frac{\Delta\cdot q}{2 p\cdot q}, \qquad \eta = \frac{Q^2}{ 2 p\cdot q}, \qquad \omega = \frac{\xi}{\eta} = \frac{q_1^2 - q_2^2}{q_1^2 + q_2^2 - \Delta^2/2} .\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-236: The transverse CF is singular at the points $z=\pm 1$ \begin{equation}C_{\perp}^{(k)}\left(z,\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right) =\frac{w C_F}{1-z}\Big[ A_\perp^{(k)}\left(z,\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right) + A_\perp^{(k)}\left(z,-\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right) \Big] +\ldots\end{equation} and the functions $A_\perp^{(2)}\left(z,\omega,\frac{Q^2 {\mu^2}\right)$ contain a series of logarithmic contributions $\sim \ln \bar z$, $\bar z = 1-z$ up to power $\ln^{2k}\bar z$.}
elements.sent-2411-237: In what follows we collect the corresponding expressions for the sum \begin{equation}\widetilde A_\perp^{(k)}\left(z,\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right) = A_\perp^{(k)}\left(z,\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right) + A_\perp^{(k)}\left(z,-\omega,\frac{Q^2}{\mu^2}\right).\end{equation}.
